notes.content: The Biochemical Engineering Bachelor of Science program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Chemical, Biochemical, Biomolecular, and Similarly Named Engineering Programs. As the biotechnology industry expands and matures, there is an increasing need for engineers who can move products from the research stage to large-scale manufacturing. As they fill this need, engineers must also understand the production, purification, and regulatory issues surrounding biopharmaceutical manufacturing. Biochemical engineers—with their strong foundations in chemistry, biological sciences, and chemical process engineering—are in a unique position to tackle these problems. Biochemical engineers apply the principles of cell and molecular biology, biochemistry, and engineering to develop, design, scale up, optimize, and operate processes that use living cells, organisms, or biological molecules for the production and purification of products (such as monoclonal antibodies, vaccines, therapeutic proteins, antibiotics, and industrial enzymes); for health and/or environmental monitoring (such as diagnostic kits, microarrays, biosensors); or for environmental improvement (such as bioremediation). An understanding of biological processes is also becoming increasingly important in the industries that traditionally employ chemical engineers, including the industries that process materials, chemicals, foods, energy, fuels, and semiconductors.
notes.contentHtml:
The Biochemical Engineering Bachelor of Science program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Chemical, Biochemical, Biomolecular, and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.


As the biotechnology industry expands and matures, there is an increasing need for engineers who can move products from the research stage to large-scale manufacturing. As they fill this need, engineers must also understand the production, purification, and regulatory issues surrounding biopharmaceutical manufacturing.


Biochemical engineers—with their strong foundations in chemistry, biological sciences, and chemical process engineering—are in a unique position to tackle these problems. Biochemical engineers apply the principles of cell and molecular biology, biochemistry, and engineering to develop, design, scale up, optimize, and operate processes that use living cells, organisms, or biological molecules for the production and purification of products (such as monoclonal antibodies, vaccines, therapeutic proteins, antibiotics, and industrial enzymes); for health and/or environmental monitoring (such as diagnostic kits, microarrays, biosensors); or for environmental improvement (such as bioremediation). An understanding of biological processes is also becoming increasingly important in the industries that traditionally employ chemical engineers, including the industries that process materials, chemicals, foods, energy, fuels, and semiconductors.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework. All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements. Important Information: Complete the courses below: ● Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A ● Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C ● Organic Chemistry (CHE) 128A/B and 129A ● Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 032A or Chemical Engineering (ENG CHM) 060 ● Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D ● Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B ● Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C ● English (ENL) 003 or University Writing Program (UWP) 001 or Comparative Literature (COM) 001 or Comparative Literature (COM) 002 or Comparative Literature (COM) 003 or Comparative Literature (COM) 004 or Native American Studies (NAS) 005 ● One of the following: ○ Engineering (ENG) 017 ○ Engineering (ENG) 035 ○ Engineering (ENG) 045 Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework.


All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below:

●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A

●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C

●     Organic Chemistry (CHE) 128A/B and 129A

●     Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 032A or Chemical Engineering (ENG CHM) 060

●     Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D

●     Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B

●     Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C

●     English (ENL) 003 or University Writing Program (UWP) 001 or Comparative Literature (COM) 001 or Comparative Literature (COM) 002 or Comparative Literature (COM) 003 or Comparative Literature (COM) 004 or Native American Studies (NAS) 005

●     One of the following:

○     Engineering (ENG) 017

○     Engineering (ENG) 035

○     Engineering (ENG) 045


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
